summary: Full backend + frontend e2e testing of new ESPACE CLIENT (login email+password) + 2-sections refactor (En attente de signature / Documents signés & disponibles) + retro-compat. Backend: 35/35 tests passing (19 new test_espace_client.py + 16 updated test_documents_espace.py — legacy assertions on chauffeur_id field were updated to destinataire_id since response shape changed). Frontend: landing page shows 3 cards (admin/chauffeur/client), client credentials generation modal displays email + 12-char password, client login OK with generated password, /client/portal shows BOTH sections with proper badges, Signer button triggers expected DocuSign 401 toast, chauffeur portal Sheet now shows renamed section labels. No critical bugs.

design_issues.screen: Admin client credentials Dialog & Chauffeur Mes documents Sheet
issues: Radix a11y warning persists: 'Missing Description or aria-describedby for DialogContent' on credentials modal. Same warning previously flagged in iteration_15; non-blocking but should add a DialogDescription / SheetDescription.

action_items: (Cosmetic) Add DialogDescription to the client-credentials Dialog in Clients.jsx and to ChauffeurPortal SheetContent to remove the Radix a11y console warning.

(Security, carry-over from iteration_15) /api/clients/{id}/generate-credentials and /revoke-credentials are NOT protected by an admin auth dependency — anyone with the URL can generate or revoke any client's password. Add Depends(get_current_admin) on these two routes (and ideally on all /documents/* routes too).

(Security) /api/documents/client/{id} and /api/documents/chauffeur/{id} are open: any caller can list the documents of any client/chauffeur by guessing the UUID. Recommend scoping the route with Depends(get_current_client) / get_current_chauffeur and asserting the path id matches the token id.
critical_code_review_comments: Open auth on the new client routes: POST /clients/{id}/generate-credentials, POST /clients/{id}/revoke-credentials, POST /client/login (login OK), and GET /documents/client/{id} are exposed without auth checks. The first two are admin-only and MUST be protected (currently anyone can reset a client's password). The GET /documents/client/{id} should require a client JWT matching the path id.
POST /client/login does not implement brute-force lockout (5 fail threshold from auth_testing.md). Admin route has it; client login should mirror the same protection.
server.py >4400 lines — still not split into routers (carry-over).
PDF upload validation: still no whitelist on content_type / extension — a non-PDF renamed .pdf would pass.

success_rate.frontend: 100% (landing 3 cards, /client/login, admin credentials modal, client portal 2 sections, sign 401 toast, chauffeur portal Sheet renamed sections)

test_credentials: Admin r.coisnon@terredebeauce.com / Mennessard03 ; Chauffeur code 43FC7D ; Client test@client.com (password regenerated each run via /generate-credentials)
seed_data_creation: Tests create/delete TEST_FE_* and TEST_* prefixed documents through fixtures with teardown. Client test@client.com (id 512b68d0-...) has its acces reactivated at end of test_espace_client.py so subsequent runs/manual checks still work. No orphan data.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend regression suites at /app/backend/tests/test_documents_espace.py (16 tests, generalized for new destinataire_* schema) and /app/backend/tests/test_espace_client.py (19 tests covering credentials generation/revocation, client login incl. case-insensitive + 401 paths, client/chauffeur document lists, sign 400/401 guards). DocuSign remains NOT authenticated in preview — /sign returning 401 with 'DocuSign non authentifié' for both chauffeur AND client docs is EXPECTED. The two client-portal sections only render when there is at least one doc in each bucket: seed a_signer + a_consulter docs (via destinataire_type=client) before testing UI. SECURITY ITEMS pending main-agent action: no admin auth on generate/revoke-credentials, no client-token check on /documents/client/{id}, no brute-force lockout on /client/login.
rca of the issue: No bug. The 4 initially-failing legacy assertions in test_documents_espace.py were due to the documents response schema migrating from `chauffeur_id` to `destinataire_id`/`destinataire_type` (intentional, generalisation of the feature) — assertions were updated to match the new contract. The 401 on /documents/{id}/sign is the expected DocuSign-not-connected guard, also surfaced as a red toast on the client and chauffeur portals — by design.
